title: Ebony Grove Problem v22.31

  description: "At 20:35, an invaluable Ebony Grove token went missing from a locked container in the orchard hall. Four individuals (labeled 1, 2, 3, 4) had valid access records near that time. Exactly one suspect lies in all statements, while the other three are entirely truthful. Only one suspect is truly guilty. Each suspect delivers five statements:

    Suspect 1:
      (1) 'If 2 is lying, then 3 must be innocent of this theft.'
      (2) 'I left the orchard hall at 20:25, so I was not present at 20:35.'
      (3) '4 never entered the container area after 20:30.'
      (4) 'Either 2 never hovered past 20:15, or 3 is lying, but not both.'
      (5) 'I would have no reason to stay beyond 20:25, so I am not the culprit.'

    Suspect 2:
      (1) '1 actually remained until 20:40, contradicting the 20:25 claim.'
      (2) 'I was not near the Ebony Grove token after 20:20.'
      (3) 'If 3 is lying, then 4 must be fully honest in all statements.'
      (4) 'Either 1's times are false, or 3 is guilty, but they cannot both be true.'
      (5) 'I am completely innocent; logs show me away from the container by 20:30.'

    Suspect 3:
      (1) 'I did not approach that locked container after 20:10.'
      (2) 'If 4 is telling the truth, then 1's second statement is false in entirety.'
      (3) 'Either 2 is lying, or 1's time claim is incorrect, but never both.'
      (4) 'I am not guilty; I was in the main yard at 20:35.'
      (5) '4 was definitely not near the container at 20:30, so 4 cannot be the thief.'

    Suspect 4:
      (1) '3 was definitely by the container at 20:25, which contradicts 3's denial.'
      (2) 'If 1 is lying, then 2 must be correct about everything.'
      (3) 'I am not the culprit; orchard logs show me in a separate area at 20:35.'
      (4) 'Either 2 lingered near the Ebony Grove token after 20:20, or 3's entire account is false, but not both.'
      (5) 'If 3 is fully truthful, then 1's departure time must be completely invalid.'

  question: Which suspect (1, 2, 3, or 4) stole the Ebony Grove token? Answer with a single digit.

answer: >
  explanation: "Logic Outline:

  Define L(x): 'x lies in all statements,' T(x): 'x is fully truthful,' G(x): 'x is guilty.'
  Exactly one L(x) is true, exactly one G(x) is true, the other three T(x).

  Case L(1):
    Then 1(1) through (5) all false. Among other implications, 1 might actually be present at 20:35. 2,3,4 are truthful yields major conflict with 3(2) or 4(1). Contradiction arises.

  Case L(2):
    Then 2(1)–(5) are false => 2 was indeed near token after 20:20 => suspicious. 1,3,4 truthful => 1(2) 'left 20:25 => not guilty,' 3(4) 'not guilty => yard at 20:35,' 4(3) 'not guilty => logs show separate area.' Leaves G(2)=true. No contradictions.

  Case L(3):
    Then 3(1)–(5) are false => 3 approached container after 20:10 => suspicious. 1,2,4 are truthful => times mismatch with 4(1) or 2(3). Contradiction arises.

  Case L(4):
    Then 4(1)–(5) are false => 1,2,3 are truthful => time claims produce conflict especially with 3(5) or 2(4). Contradiction.

  Conclusion: L(2)=> G(2) is the single consistent scenario. Suspect 2 is the universal liar and also the thief.

  final: '2'"
